skill.name: rxjs-patterns-for-angular
skill.description: Implement practical RxJS patterns for Angular applications, including Observables, operators, subscription cleanup, error handling, retry behavior, and Angular Signals interop.
skill.summary: Apply modern RxJS and Angular Signals patterns for reliable reactive Angular code.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found no executable scripts, shell calls, cryptographic code, prompt injection, or malicious intent. The remaining low risk is documentation-only network examples using Angular HttpClient, a placeholder WebSocket URL, and reference links.

low_findings.title: Documentation Network Examples
low_findings.description: The skill includes Angular HttpClient examples, one placeholder WebSocket endpoint, and external reference links. These are instructional snippets and links, not skill-executed network calls or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network strings are visible in Markdown examples and references. Confidence is high because no runnable helper script or automation invokes them.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detections
low_findings.description: The static external-command alerts point to Markdown code fences and TypeScript template snippets. No Ruby backticks, shell commands, subprocess calls, or command execution instructions were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed representative flagged sections and they are TypeScript documentation examples. There is no command interpreter context or user-input execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detections
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography alerts match Angular and RxJS terms such as Signals, toSignal, and takeUntilDestroyed. No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or cryptographic API usage appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is framework terminology, not crypto code. The surrounding context discusses reactive state and subscription cleanup.

low_findings.title: False Positive System Reconnaissance Detections
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts correspond to method names in Angular service examples, including sendMessage and loadTasks. They do not collect host, process, filesystem, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are ordinary TypeScript method examples. No local system inspection commands or APIs are present.

content.value_statement: Angular reactive code can become hard to maintain when streams, signals, and subscriptions are mixed. This skill gives focused patterns for RxJS operators, cleanup, error handling, and Signals interop.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use RxJS streams and when to use Angular Signals.
Shows patterns for toSignal, toObservable, and takeUntilDestroyed.
Covers common operators such as switchMap, mergeMap, concatMap, debounceTime, filter, and map.
Provides examples for combining streams with combineLatest, forkJoin, merge, and zip.
Documents error handling, retry, retryWhen, polling, WebSocket, and loading-state patterns.
Highlights anti-patterns such as nested subscriptions and missing unsubscribe cleanup.
limitations: It is a Markdown guidance skill, not an executable Angular library.
Examples use placeholder APIs and may need project-specific typing and services.
It does not validate code against a live Angular compiler.
It focuses on RxJS usage, not full application architecture or UI design.

use_cases.title: Modernize Angular Components
use_cases.description: Refactor component data flow from manual subscriptions to Signals and takeUntilDestroyed cleanup.

use_cases.title: Design Search and Filtering Streams
use_cases.description: Build debounced, cancellable search flows with distinctUntilChanged and switchMap.

use_cases.title: Review Reactive Code Patterns
use_cases.description: Check RxJS chains for cleanup, error handling, operator choice, and avoidable nested subscriptions.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Observable to Signal
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to convert my Angular Observable data flow into a Signal-based component pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the Right Operator
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to decide whether switchMap, mergeMap, concatMap, or debounceTime fits this Angular workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Error and Loading States
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add clear loading, error handling, retry behavior, and fallback values to this RxJS flow.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Reactive Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review this Angular feature for RxJS anti-patterns, missing cleanup, and Signals interop issues.

output_examples.input: A component subscribes manually to a service stream and updates local state.
output_examples.output: Recommendation to use takeUntilDestroyed for cleanup or convert the stream to a Signal for template use.

output_examples.input: A search box sends a request for every keystroke.
output_examples.output: Recommendation to debounce input, skip duplicate queries, and cancel stale requests with switchMap.

output_examples.input: A dashboard loads tasks, users, and settings separately.
output_examples.output: Recommendation to combine the streams into one view model and expose a stable loading and error state.

best_practices: Use Signals for template state and RxJS for async streams.
Use takeUntilDestroyed or AsyncPipe to avoid subscription leaks.
Handle errors and fallback values explicitly in every user-facing stream.
anti_patterns: Do not create nested subscriptions for dependent async work.
Do not leave manual subscriptions without lifecycle cleanup.
Do not use mergeMap when stale user-triggered requests should be cancelled.

faq.question: Does this skill run Angular or RxJS code?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for writing Angular reactive code.

faq.question: Can it help with Angular Signals?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers toSignal, toObservable, and when to combine Signals with RxJS.

faq.question: Does it replace official RxJS documentation?
faq.answer: No. It organizes common Angular patterns and links to official and learning references.

faq.question: Can it review existing code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide reviews for operator choice, cleanup, errors, and stream composition.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Angular beginners?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes basic examples, but users should know TypeScript and Angular components.

faq.question: Does it cover backend RxJS usage?
faq.answer: No. The examples focus on Angular frontend applications and Angular-specific APIs.
